name: Vowpal Wabbit (Fast Online Machine Learning)

performance.notes: Online learning — processes one example at a time; sub-millisecond per example; billions of examples feasible

agent_readiness.notes: Fast online ML for large-scale learning. import vowpalwabbit as vw. model = vw.Workspace('--sgd --l1 0.001'). ex = model.example('1 | feature1:1.0 feature2:2.0'). model.learn(ex). model.predict(ex). VW text format: label | namespace feature:value. CLI: vw --data=file.vw --model=model.vw. Algorithms: logistic regression, linear regression, LDA, contextual bandits, active learning, neural nets. Hash trick for feature expansion. Quadratic interactions: --quadratic ab. Used for click prediction, recommendation, NLP at scale. Command-line interface most common usage pattern.

gotchas: Idiosyncratic text format (VW format) vs sklearn-style API
Python interface is wrapper around C++ — ergonomics are not Pythonic
Documentation assumes knowledge of online learning concepts
Less community support vs sklearn for standard ML tasks
best_when: Streaming/online learning on billions of examples
Click prediction and real-time recommendation at web scale
Contextual bandits for online recommendation/ad systems
Large-scale linear models with hash trick feature expansion
avoid_when: Standard batch ML — sklearn much easier
Teams without online learning / VW format knowledge
When interpretability tools for sklearn models are needed
summary: Vowpal Wabbit (VW) is Microsoft/Yahoo's fast online ML library for web-scale learning. vw.Workspace('--sgd --l1 0.001') creates model. model.learn(ex) processes one example at a time. VW text format: '1 | feature:value'. Contextual bandits for real-time recommendation. Hash trick feature expansion. CLI-first usage pattern. Processes billions of examples with sub-ms latency. Idiosyncratic API — use sklearn for standard batch ML, VW for streaming/online learning at scale.
